headline: Vanuatu CONFIRMED at 35.9 Developing — UNGA climate resolution vote scheduled May 20. Per assessor instruction: DO NOT score-change this cycle (Pacific cluster pre-staged for May 21 reassessment pending vote outcome). Document the trigger event in proposal record. Final ICJ-follow-up resolution text released May 1; core support group includes Netherlands, Kenya, Singapore, Barbados, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Jamaica, Philippines. Vanuatu's INT-dimension leadership on climate is the active positive signal; domestic violence and gender gap remain negative counterweights. Internal pressure: +2.5 (sub-threshold). Re-assess May 21 if resolution passes.
pacific_cluster_pre_stage: Per assessor instruction: Pacific cluster (Vanuatu, Marshall Islands, Kiribati, Timor-Leste) is 8 days from UNGA May 20 trigger event. DO NOT score-change this cycle. Document trigger in proposal record for May 21 reassessment.

internal_reconstructed_dimensions_with_c.carry_forward_disposition: Per v1.2, +1.6 dimensional pressure preserved (SYS + INT). If UNGA May 20 resolution passes, additional positive INT signal could cross threshold for May 21 reassessment.

forward_trigger_event.event: UNGA vote on Vanuatu's ICJ Advisory Opinion follow-up climate resolution
forward_trigger_event.operationalization_target: Final text released May 1; core support group: Netherlands, Kenya, Singapore, Barbados, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Jamaica, Philippines.
forward_trigger_event.expected_scoring_impact_if_pass: Positive INT-dimension signal (+0.3-0.5); potential SYS-dimension signal (+0.2); combined with current carry-forward (+1.6), reconstructed composite could cross Functional floor at 40.0.

key_evidence: Vanuatu's UN General Assembly climate resolution scheduled for May 20 vote.
Final text released May 1; resolution operationalizes ICJ Advisory Opinion on climate obligations.
Core support group: Netherlands, Kenya, Singapore, Barbados, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Jamaica, Philippines.
Pacific students documented climate court advocacy (Amnesty, May 2026).
Vanuatu described as 'leading on climate, lagging on gender' — domestic violence endemic.
Boundary watch: 35.9 Developing; 4.1 below Functional floor (40.0) — internal reconstruction shows narrowing gap.

evidence.source: HRW — Governments should support Vanuatu's UN climate resolution
Climate Change News — ICJ follow-up resolution is a test of climate leadership at the UN
evidence.finding: May 20 vote; final text released; support group documented.

evidence.source: Amnesty — How students from the Pacific took the fight against climate change to the world's top court
evidence.finding: Pacific climate advocacy origins and impact.

scoring_rationale.EQU: 2 unchanged. Domestic violence and gender gap remain.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 unchanged at published. Internal +0.2: ICJ Advisory Opinion operationalization is S1 Root Cause Orientation + S4 Structural Critique signal.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 unchanged at published. Internal +0.2: sustained climate leadership at scale-costly diplomatic infrastructure.

math_check.validator_check: Confirmation at 35.9 Developing. +1.6 internal carry-forward pressure logged. Pre-staged for May 21 reassessment.

negativeSignals: Domestic violence endemic; gender gap; frequent natural disasters; lagging on gender despite climate leadership.
positiveSignals: UNGA climate resolution May 20 vote; ICJ Advisory Opinion operationalization; broad coalition support; Pacific student-advocacy origin story.
sector_context: Pacific climate cluster — Vanuatu is the diplomatic anchor. Marshall Islands, Kiribati, Timor-Leste in supporting boundary positions. UNGA May 20 vote could trigger multiple Pacific upgrades.
open_actions_resolved: Pacific cluster instruction respected (no score change this cycle)
methodology_consistency_check: Pre-stage documentation only. No score change per assessor instruction. v1.2 carry-forward applied. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
